name: nft-standards
description: Implement NFT standards (ERC-721, ERC-1155) with proper metadata handling, minting strategies, and marketplace integration. Use when creating NFT contracts, building NFT marketplaces, or implementing digital asset systems.
capabilities: Explain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 standard differences
Provide Solidity examples for minting and supply limits
Describe off-chain and on-chain metadata schemas
Show patterns for soulbound, dynamic, and ERC721A NFTs
author: wshobson
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation (SKILL.md) and JSON configuration files (skill-report.json). No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access beyond its own files, or environment variable reads are present. All 50 static findings are false positives triggered by example Solidity code blocks in documentation. The flagged patterns (C2 keywords, weak crypto, dynamic require, shell backticks) are standard blockchain terminology and documentation formatting, not security threats. Users are not exposed to any data theft, code execution, or privacy risks.
valueStatement: NFT builders need clear guidance on standards, metadata, and minting. This skill gives practical patterns for ERC-721, ERC-1155, and marketplace readiness.
userTitle: Implement NFT standards with ERC-721 and ERC-1155
limitations: Does not deploy contracts or run blockchain transactions
Does not upload assets to IPFS or manage pinning
Does not perform security audits of custom contracts
Does not integrate with marketplace APIs

useCases.title: Select a token standard
useCases.description: Compare ERC-721 and ERC-1155 for a new collection and choose the right model.

useCases.title: Design metadata schema
useCases.description: Define fields that display correctly across major NFT marketplaces and wallets.

useCases.title: Plan multi-token items
useCases.description: Set ERC-1155 item IDs, supplies, and mint rules for game assets.

promptTemplates.title: Choose ERC standard
promptTemplates.prompt: Recommend ERC-721 or ERC-1155 for a 5,000 item art drop and explain tradeoffs.

promptTemplates.title: Metadata checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: List required and optional metadata fields for NFT JSON and note best practices for images and attributes.

promptTemplates.title: Royalties policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to implement EIP-2981 royalties and suggest a safe fee cap.

promptTemplates.title: Dynamic NFT design
promptTemplates.prompt: Outline a dynamic NFT approach that updates metadata based on on-chain state changes.

bestPractices: Use OpenZeppelin base contracts for standards compliance
Keep metadata consistent and pinned on IPFS
Document mint limits and royalty settings
antiPatterns: Mixing ERC-721 and ERC-1155 without a clear need
Changing metadata without a disclosure policy
Storing large assets fully on-chain without cost justification

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance and examples that work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It does not deploy contracts, run transactions, or audit your code.

faq.question: Does it integrate with marketplace APIs?
faq.answer: No. It explains standards and metadata, but it does not call marketplace services.

faq.question: Does it access my files or keys?
faq.answer: No. It is static guidance and does not access local files or credentials.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot missing metadata display?
faq.answer: Verify tokenURI format, JSON fields, and IPFS pinning status.

faq.question: How does it compare to a full audit tool?
faq.answer: It offers design guidance only, not automated testing or formal security review.
